questions.prompt: A student pays $1,260 for a 6-credit course. What is the cost per credit?
questions.explanation: Divide the total cost by the number of credits: $1,260 ÷ 6 = $210 per credit.

questions.prompt: Solve for x: 7x − 12 = 3x + 24
questions.explanation: Subtract 3x from both sides to get 4x − 12 = 24. Add 12 to get 4x = 36, so x = 9.

questions.prompt: A rectangular courtyard is 18 meters long and 11 meters wide. A walkway is built along one long side and one short side of the courtyard. How many meters of walkway are needed?
questions.explanation: The walkway covers one long side and one short side, so the total length is 18 + 11 = 29 meters.

questions.prompt: The table shows the number of survey responses collected by a student group over four days. Monday: 34 Tuesday: 41 Wednesday: 37 Thursday: 48 What is the mean number of responses collected per day?
questions.explanation: The total is 34 + 41 + 37 + 48 = 160. Dividing by 4 gives a mean of 40.

questions.prompt: A meal plan provides 18 lunches for $153. At this rate, what would be the cost of 25 lunches?
questions.explanation: The cost per lunch is $153 ÷ 18 = $8.50. For 25 lunches, the cost is 25 × $8.50 = $212.50.

questions.prompt: Which expression is equivalent to 3(4y + 5) − 2(3y − 7)?
questions.explanation: Distribute and combine like terms: 12y + 15 − 6y + 14 = 6y + 29.

questions.prompt: A circular table has a circumference of 16π inches. What is the diameter of the table?
questions.explanation: The circumference of a circle is C = πd. If C = 16π, then d = 16 inches.

questions.prompt: A bag contains 9 yellow beads, 6 purple beads, and 5 silver beads. One bead is selected at random. What is the probability that the bead is yellow or silver?
questions.explanation: There are 9 + 6 + 5 = 20 beads total. Yellow or silver beads total 9 + 5 = 14, so the probability is 14/20 = 7/10.

questions.prompt: A store increases the price of a desk from $160 to $188. What is the percent increase?
questions.explanation: The increase is $188 − $160 = $28. The percent increase is 28/160 = 0.175, or 17.5%.

questions.prompt: A tutoring center charges $18 for registration and $16 for each tutoring session. If a student pays $114 in total, how many tutoring sessions did the student attend?
questions.explanation: Subtract the registration fee: 114 − 18 = 96. Divide by the session cost: 96 ÷ 16 = 6 sessions.

questions.prompt: A triangular lot has side lengths of 13 yards, 14 yards, and 15 yards. Its perimeter will be fenced. The fence costs $9 per yard. What is the total cost of the fence?
questions.explanation: The perimeter is 13 + 14 + 15 = 42 yards. At $9 per yard, the cost is 42 × 9 = $378.

questions.prompt: The data set shows the numbers of minutes a student spent exercising on seven days: 20, 25, 25, 30, 35, 40, 70 Which statement best describes the effect of the value 70 on the data set?

choices.A: It increases the mean more than it increases the median.
choices.B: It increases the median more than it increases the mean.
choices.C: It makes the mean and median equal.
choices.D: It decreases the range of the data set.

questions.correctAnswerText: It increases the mean more than it increases the median.
questions.explanation: The value 70 is much larger than the other values, so it pulls the mean upward. The median remains the middle value, 30.

questions.prompt: A package weighs 4.8 pounds. The shipping company charges $2.35 per pound, rounded to the nearest pound before calculating the charge. What is the shipping charge?
questions.explanation: The weight 4.8 pounds rounds to 5 pounds. The charge is 5 × $2.35 = $11.75.

questions.prompt: Which ordered pair is a solution to the system of equations? y = 2x − 1 y = −x + 8
questions.explanation: Substitute x = 3 into both equations: y = 2(3) − 1 = 5 and y = −3 + 8 = 5, so (3, 5) satisfies both equations.

questions.prompt: A rectangular room is 15 feet long and 12 feet wide. A scale drawing of the room uses 1 inch to represent 3 feet. What are the dimensions of the room on the scale drawing?
questions.explanation: Divide each actual dimension by 3: 15 ÷ 3 = 5 inches and 12 ÷ 3 = 4 inches.

questions.prompt: A committee has 5 first-year students and 7 second-year students. If two students are selected at random without replacement, what is the probability that both students are second-year students?
questions.explanation: The probability of selecting a second-year student first is 7/12. Without replacement, the probability of selecting a second-year student second is 6/11. The product is 7/12 × 6/11 = 42/132 = 7/22.

questions.prompt: A phone battery drops from 92% charged to 56% charged in 3 hours. If the battery charge decreases at the same average rate, how many percentage points does it drop per hour?
questions.explanation: The charge drops by 92 − 56 = 36 percentage points over 3 hours. The average drop is 36 ÷ 3 = 12 percentage points per hour.

questions.prompt: Which expression is equivalent to x² − 16?
questions.explanation: x² − 16 is a difference of squares: x² − 4² = (x − 4)(x + 4).

questions.prompt: A point is located at (−4, 6). It is moved 7 units to the right and 9 units down. What are the coordinates of the new point?
questions.explanation: Moving 7 units right changes the x-coordinate from −4 to 3. Moving 9 units down changes the y-coordinate from 6 to −3.

questions.prompt: A small business tracks the number of orders it receives during five weekdays. Monday: 16 Tuesday: 19 Wednesday: 23 Thursday: 23 Friday: 29 Which statement is true?
questions.explanation: The value 23 appears twice, more often than any other value in the data set.
